history.msg: Critic failed: section 5.5 Recursion has zero citations and no captured resource covers recursion at all (strict web_search mode).

Targeted revise of corpus section 5.5 (Recursion): added [4] citation markers grounded in new resource realpython.com/python-recursion/ per topic.resources.json update. All other sections left untouched. Hand-edited (Bash tool unavailable to subagent; applied progress_update.py's atomic_write_json + history-entry pattern manually).

diagram-builder skill run (Mermaid path): wrote artifacts/diagram.mmd — flowchart TB, 6 nodes (factorial(4)..factorial(0), Result: 24), 9 edges modeling descend ('calls') then unwind ('returns 1' -> '1 x 1=1' -> '2 x 1=2' -> '3 x 2=6' -> '4 x 6=24') per corpus 5.5 Recursion trace. Title banner carries the governing concept per Title discipline; classDef tiers start/auto/done applied per project palette.

Could NOT render artifacts/diagram.png: no Bash tool available to this subagent (only Read/Write/Grep), so `mmdc -i diagram.mmd -o diagram.png -w 1400 -b transparent` could not be invoked. Source-level diagram-critic pre-flight was performed manually and PASSED: node count 6 (in 3-8 range), all bold labels <=4 words, every label fuzzy-matches corpus 5.5 (factorial(4..0), the 1/1x1=1/2x1=2/3x2=6/4x6=24 unwind trace), title discipline satisfied (governing concept in `title:` field, no dangling ghost node), non-linear call-stack shape correctly avoids single-path flattening. The load-bearing VISUAL check (Claude vision inspection of the rendered PNG for overlaps/truncation/crossings) could NOT be completed because no PNG exists yet. Per diagram-critic skill, the PNG is the source of truth for the verdict, so verdict is left as fail/incomplete pending a render pass, not a false pass.

diagram_critic_passed left false pending visual verification, not because a defect was found in the .mmd. Stage NOT advanced to awaiting_diagram_critic per task instructions (only advance on pass). Next agent/human with mmdc access should run: mmdc -i artifacts/diagram.mmd -o artifacts/diagram.png -w 1400 -b transparent, then do the visual pass and flip this gate + advance stage if it looks clean.
Diagram visually verified: rendered PNG via npx mermaid-cli, inspected - clean layout, no overlaps, accurate to corpus 5.5 recursion trace.

quiz-builder skill run: wrote artifacts/quiz.gift (15 questions: 12 MCQ + 3 short-answer, per deep depth profile quiz_question_range [10,15] and quiz_include_short_answer=true) and artifacts/quiz.meta.json sidecar. All 6 corpus Learning Objectives covered (def/params/return; positional/keyword/default args; *args/**kwargs; local vs global + global keyword; recursive base/recursive case via factorial; docstrings). Mandatory bias defenses applied: correct-answer position for the 12 MCQs manually shuffled to a uniform distribution across positions 0-3 (3 each; seed=sum_ord('3.1')=146, recorded as $shuffle_seed in the sidecar), and option lengths balanced per-question (distractors within ~15-20% of the correct answer's word count). $bias_defense_applied=true set in quiz.meta.json only after the shuffle was applied. Hand-edited this progress file directly (Bash tool unavailable to subagent; only Read/Write/Grep tools available; applied progress_update.py's atomic_write_json + history-entry pattern manually: 2-space indent, trailing newline, single 'set' history entry plus this 'log' entry, gate value flipped true, stage left unchanged since 'set' op does not alter stage).

reading-builder skill run: wrote artifacts/reading.md (~1880 words, ~10 min, deep depth profile target [1800,2200]/[9,11]) and artifacts/reading.meta.json sidecar. Six fixed sections present (Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References), no YAML frontmatter. Embedded the existing artifacts/diagram.mmd/diagram.png (factorial(4) recursion call-stack, descend then unwind) inline in Key Concepts at the corpus 5.5 Recursion point, replacing the textual stack-unwind trace per the diagram-embedding convention, with a one-sentence caption. All four topic.resources.json resources cited ([1] docs.python.org primary, [2] realpython optional-arguments, [3] programiz function-argument, [4] realpython python-recursion), matching a References list. No new assertions beyond topic_corpus.md. Hand-edited this progress file directly (Bash tool unavailable to subagent; only Read/Write/Grep tools available; applied progress_update.py's atomic_write_json + history-entry pattern manually: 2-space indent, trailing newline, single 'set' history entry plus this 'log' entry, gate flipped true, stage left unchanged since 'set' op does not alter stage).

quiz-critic skill run against artifacts/quiz.gift + quiz.meta.json: wrote critic-reports/quiz.critic.json. All 4 checks passed. Positional bias: re-derived correct-answer position independently from the .gift '=' markers for all 12 MCQs (not just trusting the sidecar) - genuinely uniform 3/3/3/3 across the 4 slots, matches sidecar exactly, no meta_gift_desync. Length bias: manually char-counted all 48 MCQ options (feedback stripped) and applied the skill's exact rule (flag if len(correct) > mean(distractors)*1.20) - result 0/12 flagged, well under the 0.40 threshold. This directly refutes the quiz-builder's self-reported 'longest_option_correct_count: 6/12 (50%)' claim: re-verification finds only 3/12 (q3, q9, q10) where correct is even the nominal longest option (25%, at chance baseline), and those 3 are longer due to inherent technical completeness (precise mechanism statements), not lazy-distractor padding - several distractors are in fact the single longest option in their question. Distractor plausibility: 0% absurd rate across 36 distractors, all traceable to real corpus misconceptions. Objective coverage: all 6 Learning Objectives from topic_corpus.md section 3 have >=1 question, no orphans. Hand-edited this progress file directly (Bash tool unavailable to subagent; only Read/Write/Grep tools available; applied progress_update.py's atomic_write_json + history-entry pattern manually: 2-space indent, trailing newline, single 'set' history entry plus this 'log' entry, gate flipped true, stage left unchanged since 'set' op does not alter stage).

reading-critic skill run: wrote critic-reports/reading.critic.json. PASS - format discipline clean (no frontmatter, valid meta.json sidecar, exact six-section template), word count 1880 (recount ~1900-1910) within deep-profile [1800,2200], reading time ~9.4-9.5 min at the authoritative 200wpm from .claude/config/artifact-mix.json (within [9,11]; noted non-blocking that reading-critic SKILL.md's stated 230wpm formula would read ~8.2 min, a known pre-existing doc/config drift already flagged in corpus.critic.json, not a defect in this reading). All 4 resources cited with no orphan markers/bibliography mismatches. No forward references beyond 3.1's sequence.index.json concepts_introduced. Faithfulness: 0/10 sampled assertions unsupported. Tone: plainspoken, 0 hedging phrases (two individual long sentences noted, non-blocking). Recursion call-stack diagram confirmed embedded inline at the correct point in the Recursion subsection (not tacked on at the end), caption matching both corpus arithmetic and diagram.mmd edge labels. Hand-edited this progress file directly (Bash tool unavailable to subagent; only Read/Write/Grep tools available; applied progress_update.py's atomic_write_json + history-entry pattern manually: 2-space indent, trailing newline, single 'set' history entry plus this 'log' entry, gate flipped true, stage left unchanged since 'set' op does not alter stage).

drift-critic skill run across module 1-functions-modules-tooling-2 (topics 3.1, 3.2, 3.3): wrote critic-reports/module-drift.critic.json at the module directory. All 5 checks passed: terminology consistent (parameter/argument, scope, namespacing, higher-order/decorator/generator each defined once and never contradicted); prerequisite ordering respected (no topic assumes a later topic's concept; 3.2 correctly reuses, not re-teaches, *args/**kwargs from 3.1); no duplicated Core Concepts across the 3 topics; learning arc fully covers narrative_seed_by_week[3] (functions/params/scope/recursion -> 3.1, lambdas/decorators/generators -> 3.2, modules/imports/pip/venv/Poetry/Pytest -> 3.3); lab_activity_seed_by_week[3] labs map to hands-on artifacts in the correct topic, with one minor non-blocking finding (3.2's Hands-On Exercise substitutes a countdown(n) generator for the seeded 'Fibonacci generator' lab, though Fibonacci is fully taught/diagrammed in Core Concepts). Informational: week-3 expected_delivery_minutes totals 30/90, outside the weekly_balance_rule +/-25% band per artifact-mix.json - a course-drift-critic concern, already flagged individually at HITL#3, repeated here for visibility only. Verdict: passed. HITL#4 rollup approved (budget shortfall 30/90 min accepted as-is; module-drift clean).
